title: Execution phase
description: A phase of the execution roadmap with goal, scope, deliverables, validation, risks, and exit criteria. IDs follow the PH-N scheme.

goal.description: The outcome this phase exists to achieve.

scope.description: What is in (and explicitly out of) scope for this phase.

deliverables.description: Concrete outputs produced by the phase.

validation.description: How the phase's work is validated before exit.

risks.description: Risks most relevant to this phase.

exit_criteria.description: Conditions that must all be true to consider the phase complete (the gate to the next phase).

depends_on.description: Phases that must complete before this one starts.

work_items.description: WBS work items realized in this phase.

acceptance_refs.description: Acceptance criteria (AC-) this phase is responsible for satisfying. They are verified (verdict + evidence) in the acceptance audit (validation/acceptance-audit.md) at the phase gate.
